docs.heroDesc: Integrate Sakura Account into your app, enabling unified identity authentication in minutes
docs.quickStartDesc: Follow a few simple steps to add OAuth2.0 / OIDC authentication to your app
docs.step1Desc: Register your application in the Sakura Account dashboard to get a Client ID and Client Secret
docs.step2Desc: Set your redirect URI to ensure the callback after authorization works correctly
docs.step3Desc: Redirect users to the authorization page; after consent, you'll receive an authorization code
docs.step4Desc: Use the authorization code to exchange for an Access Token and Refresh Token at the Token endpoint
docs.step5Desc: Use the Access Token to call the UserInfo endpoint and retrieve the user's profile
docs.featureOAuthDesc: Industry-standard authorization protocol supporting Authorization Code, Client Credentials, and more
docs.featureOIDCDesc: Identity layer on top of OAuth 2.0 providing standardized user identity information
docs.featureSSODesc: One login grants access to all authorized applications, improving user experience
docs.featurePasskeyDesc: WebAuthn-based biometric and hardware key authentication—more secure and convenient
docs.featureJWTDesc: JWT-formatted ID Tokens and Access Tokens with built-in signature verification
docs.featureScopesDesc: Control app access to user data through scopes, following the principle of least privilege
docs.authFlowDesc: Standard OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code + PKCE flow
docs.flowStep2: Browser redirects to Sakura Account authorization page
docs.flowStep3: User logs in and grants consent
docs.flowStep4: Authorization code returned via callback to your app
docs.flowStep5: App backend exchanges auth code for tokens
docs.flowStep6: Use tokens to retrieve user information
docs.endpointsDesc: All endpoints return standard JSON and follow OAuth 2.0 / OIDC specifications
docs.epAuthorizeDesc: User authorization entry point, supports OAuth 2.0 and OIDC requests
docs.epTokenDesc: Exchange authorization codes, refresh tokens, or client credentials for tokens
docs.epUserInfoDesc: Retrieve the authenticated user's identity information
docs.epOpenIdConfigDesc: OIDC Discovery endpoint returning all related endpoints and metadata
docs.epJwksDesc: JSON Web Key Set—public keys for verifying JWT signatures
docs.epRevokeDesc: Revoke previously issued access or refresh tokens
docs.authCodeDesc: The recommended secure authorization flow for apps with a backend
docs.clientCredentialsDesc: For service-to-service and machine-to-machine authentication scenarios
docs.refreshTokenDesc: Obtain a new Access Token after the current one expires using a Refresh Token
docs.clientIdDesc: The Client ID obtained when registering your app
docs.scopeDesc: Requested permission scopes, e.g. openid profile email. Optional — defaults to the app's registered base scopes when omitted. Must include openid if using OIDC.
docs.stateDesc: Random string to prevent CSRF attacks. Required in all requests.
docs.codeChallengeDesc: S256 hash for PKCE to prevent authorization code interception
docs.codeChallengeMethodDesc: PKCE challenge method. Optional — defaults to S256 when omitted. Only S256 is accepted; plain is not supported.
docs.promptDesc: Controls the authorization page behavior. Optional — if omitted, the system auto-determines whether to show the consent screen (skips if already logged in and previously authorized). Pass consent to force re-confirmation.
docs.codeExamplesDesc: Integration examples in various languages and frameworks to help you get started
docs.securityDesc: To protect your app and user data, please follow these security recommendations
docs.secStateDesc: Generate a random state value and verify it in callbacks to effectively prevent CSRF attacks
docs.secPKCEDesc: Even in apps with backend services, enabling PKCE adds an extra layer of security
docs.secSecretDesc: Never expose your Client Secret in client-side code or public repositories—store it securely on the backend only
docs.secValidateDesc: Verify ID Token signatures using JWKS public keys, and validate claims such as iss, aud, and exp
docs.secHTTPSDesc: All communication must use HTTPS to protect tokens and user data in transit
docs.secRotationDesc: Periodically update Client Secrets and signing keys to reduce exposure risk
docs.scopesDesc: Control the scope of app access to user data through the scope parameter
docs.scopeProfileInfo: Get profile information such as nickname and avatar
docs.notePkce: PKCE (S256) is mandatory — code_challenge is required. code_challenge_method defaults to S256 if omitted.
docs.noteRedirectUri: redirect_uri must exactly match the callback URL configured in your app settings
docs.noteTokenExpiry: Default Access Token lifetime is 1 hour; Refresh Token lifetime is 30 days
docs.faqDesc: Common questions and answers encountered during integration
docs.faqQ1: Which OAuth 2.0 grant flows does Sakura Account support?
docs.faqA1: We currently support Authorization Code, Client Credentials, and Refresh Token flows. PKCE is enabled by default for enhanced security.
docs.faqQ2: How long is the Access Token valid? What happens when it expires?
docs.faqA2: Access Tokens default to a 1-hour lifetime. After expiration, you can use the Refresh Token to obtain a new Access Token without requiring the user to re-authorize.
docs.faqQ3: Can I customize the login page appearance?
docs.faqA3: You can set a custom logo and primary color via optional OAuth request parameters. Contact us for more customization options.
docs.faqQ4: Can Passkey authentication replace traditional passwords?
docs.faqA4: Yes. Sakura Account supports WebAuthn/Passkey standards, allowing users to log in password-free with fingerprints, face recognition, or hardware keys—more secure than passwords.
docs.faqQ5: How do I migrate existing users to Sakura Account?
docs.faqA5: We provide user import APIs and progressive migration strategies. You can gradually migrate users while maintaining support for your existing account system.
docs.faqQ6: Which programming languages have official SDKs?
docs.faqA6: Currently we offer REST APIs—any OAuth 2.0 / OIDC compliant generic SDK works directly. Official SDKs are under development.
docs.conclusionDesc: Register your first app and integrate within minutes

docs.simWelcomeSubtitle: Sign in to your account to manage identity and application access.
docs.simAppDesc: A demo app using Sakura Account for authentication

docs.simLoginHint: Enter any username and password to try
docs.simScopeProfileDesc: Access your nickname, avatar and basic info
docs.simDemoComplete: Demo complete — here is the user info returned to the app
docs.simDeniedDesc: You denied the authorization request for this app
docs.simPasskeyNotSupported: Passkey login is not supported in demo mode
